Motion For Entry Of Judgment Under Code Of Civil Procedure 664.6`
Off Calendar Dec-20-2023 Continued To Jan-09-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jacob Harker, Esq., LAW OFFICES OF JACOB HARKER (415) 624-7602 jacob@harkercounsel.com Attorneys for Plaintiff Nace Reynolds
Connie J.Parchman CSR #6137, 510-853-5260, parchman1@gmail.com, Court Reporter
No other apperances.

Not ARGUED. . The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF NACE REYNOLDS Motion For Entry Of Judgment Under Code Of Civil Procedure 664.6

Plaintiff's motion for entry of judgment is continued to January 9, 2024.

The written settlement is admissible under Evidence Code sec. 1123(b) since it provides that it is "CCP 664.6 enforceable." (Harker Decl., Ex. A.) The terms of the parties' Long-Form Release, confidentiality, and non-disparagement agreements are sufficiently definite to be enforced. After entering into their initial settlement, they agreed on the terms of those covenants. (Harker Reply Decl., pars. 6-12.) This motion, however, is premature. The settlement provides that payment is due "within 30 days of fully-executed agreement & W9." The evidence indicates that plaintiff executed and served the "fully-executed agreement" on November 22, 2023. (Id. at 10; Schoe Decl., par. 4 & Exs.1 and 2.)

The motion is therefore continued. The court anticipates that the parties will resolve this dispute prior to the next hearing, court intervention will no longer be necessary, and moving party will take this motion off calendar prior to the new hearing date.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter:Connie J.Parchman CSR #6137, 510-853-5260, parchman1@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
